headline: Special Forces Expand With 2 New Battalions Added
context: Malaysian Armed Forces announced 2 new special forces battalions in December 2025 increasing capability by 40%.

cards.big: Military forms 2 new special forces battalions increasing capability 40%
cards.sub: Expansion addresses counter-terrorism maritime and jungle warfare gaps

cards.big: New battalions add 1,200 operators to existing 3,000-strong contingent
cards.sub: Recruitment targets graduates with technical skills for cyber and intelligence

cards.big: Training includes 18 months with rotations to US UK and Australian units
cards.sub: RM280 million allocated for equipment and training over 3-year establishment

cards.big: One battalion based in Sabah specifically for eastern maritime security
cards.sub: Unit specializes in interdiction and hostage rescue in littoral environment

cards.big: Special forces expansion tests whether elite capability addresses Borneo security requirements
Two new special forces battalions expand capability. Force structure growth must match threat assessment, not prestige.
